what_it_does: Defines a single RELATION_TYPE dict declaring a directed board edge 'blocked_by' meaning X is blocked by Y — a dependency/gating relation between addressed perspectives or claims on the board.
observations: desc frames it as a CONCEPTUAL edge between addressed perspectives/claims (the harvest's RELATIONS), first-class on the board.
Explicitly says the linking layer parses it rather than collapsing it to 'references'.
Notes it is surfaced by-use during the wind-down harvest (recollection).
standouts: Insistence that this dependency edge stay first-class and not be collapsed into a generic 'references' relation.
conventions: Registry-style: one file declaring one RELATION_TYPE dict with id/directed/label/desc.
desc carries the conceptual rationale and lifecycle (surfaced during wind-down harvest).
notes: A directed board relation type for dependency/gating edges between claims, kept distinct from generic references.
questions: What is the 'wind-down harvest (recollection)' process that surfaces these edges?
What is the full set of sibling board_edges relation types this sits among?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares a blocked-by relation type and the desc explains exactly that.
apparent_themes: first-class typed relations (no collapse to generic references)
board as a graph of claims/perspectives
intention_signals: Trying to preserve the semantic distinction of dependency/gating between claims so the board's linking layer can reason about blockers explicitly.
contribution: Contributes the directed dependency/gating relation type 'blocked_by' to the board edge vocabulary, with its conceptual definition and harvest-surfaced lifecycle.
summary_for_embedding: A directed board relation type 'blocked_by' declaring X-is-blocked-by-Y dependency edges between addressed claims/perspectives.
intention_for_embedding: To keep dependency/gating between claims a first-class typed edge on the board rather than a generic reference.
